data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_NEWDLC
data.value: New Downloadable Content is available! Access it from the Minecraft Store button on the Main Menu.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_SKINPACKS
data.value: You can change the look of your character with a Skin Pack from the Minecraft Store. Select 'Minecraft Store' on the Main Menu to see what's available.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_2
data.value: Alter the gamma settings to make the game brighter or darker.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_3
data.value: If you set the game difficulty to Peaceful, your health will automatically regenerate, and no monsters will come out at night!

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_4
data.value: Feed a bone to a wolf to tame it. You can then make it sit or follow you.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_5
data.value: You can drop items when in the Inventory menu by moving the cursor off the menu and pressing{*CONTROLLER_VK_A*}

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_6
data.value: Sleeping in a bed at night will fast forward the game to dawn, but all players in a multiplayer game need to sleep in beds at the same time.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_7
data.value: Harvest pork chops from pigs, and cook and eat them to regain health.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_8
data.value: Harvest leather from cows, and use it to make armor.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_9
data.value: If you have an empty bucket, you can fill it with milk from a cow, or water, or lava!

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_10
data.value: Use a hoe to prepare areas of ground for planting.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_11
data.value: Spiders won't attack you during the day - unless you attack them.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_12
data.value: Digging soil or sand with a spade is faster than with your hand!

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_13
data.value: Eating cooked pork chops gives more health than eating raw pork chops.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_14
data.value: Make some torches to light up areas at night. Monsters will avoid the areas around these torches.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_15
data.value: Get to destinations faster with a minecart and rail!

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_16
data.value: Plant some saplings and they'll grow into trees.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_17
data.value: Pigmen won't attack you, unless you attack them.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_18
data.value: You can change your game spawn point and skip to dawn by sleeping in a bed.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_19
data.value: Hit those fireballs back at the Ghast!

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_20
data.value: Building a portal will allow you to travel to another dimension - The Nether.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_21
data.value: Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_B*} to drop the item currently in your hand!

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_22
data.value: Use the right tool for the job!

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_23
data.value: If you can't find any coal for your torches, you can always make charcoal from trees in a furnace.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_24
data.value: Digging straight down or straight up is not a great idea.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_25
data.value: Bonemeal (crafted from a Skeleton bone) can be used as a fertilizer, and can make things grow instantly!

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_26
data.value: Creepers explode when they get close to you!

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_27
data.value: Obsidian is created when water hits a lava source block.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_28
data.value: Lava can take minutes to disappear COMPLETELY when the source block is removed.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_29
data.value: Cobblestone is resistant to Ghast fireballs, making it useful for guarding portals.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_30
data.value: Blocks that can be used as a light source will melt snow and ice. This includes torches, glowstone, and Jack-O-Lanterns.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_31
data.value: Take caution when building structures made of wool in open air, as lightning from thunderstorms can set wool on fire.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_32
data.value: A single bucket of lava can be used in a furnace to smelt 100 blocks.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_33
data.value: The instrument played by a note block depends on the material beneath it.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_34
data.value: Zombies and Skeletons can survive daylight if they are in water.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_35
data.value: Attacking a wolf will cause any wolves in the immediate vicinity to turn hostile and attack you. This trait is also shared by Zombie Pigmen.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_38
data.value: Chickens lay an egg every 5 to 10 minutes.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_39
data.value: Obsidian can only be mined with a diamond pickaxe.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_40
data.value: Creepers are the easiest obtainable source of gunpowder.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_41
data.value: Placing two chests side by side will make one large chest.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_42
data.value: Tame wolves show their health with the position of their tail. Feed them meat to heal them.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_43
data.value: Cook cactus in a furnace to get green dye.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_46
data.value: Read the What's New section in the How To Play menus to see the latest update information about the game.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_47
data.value: Stackable fences are in the game now!

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_49
data.value: Some animals will follow you if you have wheat in your hand.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_GAMETIP_50
data.value: If an animal can't move more than 20 blocks in any direction, it won't despawn.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_TRIVIA_2
data.value: Notch has over a million followers on twitter!

data.name: IDS_TIPS_TRIVIA_3
data.value: Not all Swedish people have blonde hair. Some, like Jens from Mojang, even have ginger hair!

data.name: IDS_TIPS_TRIVIA_5
data.value: There will be an update to this game eventually!

data.name: IDS_TIPS_TRIVIA_7
data.value: Mojang has more awards than staff!

data.name: IDS_TIPS_TRIVIA_10
data.value: Do not look directly at the bugs.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_TRIVIA_11
data.value: Creepers were born from a coding bug.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_TRIVIA_12
data.value: Is it a chicken or is it a duck?

data.name: IDS_TIPS_TRIVIA_14
data.value: No-one at Mojang has ever seen junkboy's face.

data.name: IDS_TIPS_TRIVIA_15
data.value: Did you know there's a Minecraft Wiki?

data.name: IDS_TIPS_TRIVIA_18
data.value: Minecon 2013 was in Orlando, Florida, USA!

data.name: IDS_TIPS_TRIVIA_20
data.value: Always assume rumors are false, rather than assuming they're true!

data.name: IDS_HOW_TO_PLAY_BASICS
IDS_HOW_TO_PLAY_HUD

IDS_HOW_TO_PLAY_FARMANIMALS
data.value: {*T3*}HOW TO PLAY : FARMING ANIMALS{*ETW*}{*B*}{*B*} If you want to keep your animals in the one place, build a fenced area of less than 20x20 blocks and have your animals inside it. This ensures they will still be there when you come back to see them.

data.name: IDS_HOW_TO_PLAY_BREEDANIMALS
IDS_HOW_TO_PLAY_NETHERPORTAL
IDS_HOW_TO_PLAY_BANLIST
data.value: {*T3*}HOW TO PLAY : BANNING LEVELS{*ETW*}{*B*}{*B*} If you find offensive content within a level you are playing, you can choose to add the level to your Banned Levels list. If you would like to do this, bring up the Pause menu, then press{*CONTROLLER_VK_RB*} to select the Ban Level tooltip. When you attempt to join this level in future, you will be notified that the level is in your Banned Levels list, and given the option to remove it from the list and continue into the level, or back out.

data.name: IDS_HOW_TO_PLAY_HOSTOPTIONS
IDS_HOW_TO_PLAY_THEEND
IDS_HOW_TO_PLAY_WHATSNEW
IDS_TITLEUPDATE
IDS_HOW_TO_PLAY_HORSES
IDS_HOW_TO_PLAY_BEACONS
IDS_HOW_TO_PLAY_FIREWORKS
IDS_HOW_TO_PLAY_HOPPERS
IDS_HOW_TO_PLAY_DROPPERS
IDS_DESC_SWORD
data.value: Deals more damage than by hand.

data.name: IDS_DESC_SHOVEL
data.value: Used to dig dirt, grass, sand, gravel and snow faster than by hand. Shovels are required to dig snowballs.

data.name: IDS_DESC_PICKAXE
data.value: Required to mine stone-related blocks and ore.

data.name: IDS_DESC_HATCHET
data.value: Used to chop wood-related blocks faster than by hand.

data.name: IDS_DESC_HOE
data.value: Used to till dirt and grass blocks to prepare for crops.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DOOR_WOOD
data.value: Wooden doors are activated by using, hitting them or with Redstone.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DOOR_IRON
data.value: Iron doors can only be opened by Redstone, buttons or switches.

data.name: IDS_DESC_HELMET_LEATHER
data.value: Gives the user 1 Armor when worn.

data.name: IDS_DESC_CHESTPLATE_LEATHER
data.value: Gives the user 3 Armor when worn.

data.name: IDS_DESC_LEGGINGS_LEATHER
data.value: Gives the user 2 Armor when worn.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BOOTS_LEATHER
data.value: Gives the user 1 Armor when worn.

data.name: IDS_DESC_HELMET_CHAIN
data.value: Gives the user 2 Armor when worn.

data.name: IDS_DESC_CHESTPLATE_CHAIN
data.value: Gives the user 5 Armor when worn.

data.name: IDS_DESC_LEGGINGS_CHAIN
data.value: Gives the user 4 Armor when worn.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BOOTS_CHAIN
data.value: Gives the user 1 Armor when worn.

data.name: IDS_DESC_HELMET_IRON
data.value: Gives the user 2 Armor when worn.

data.name: IDS_DESC_CHESTPLATE_IRON
data.value: Gives the user 6 Armor when worn.

data.name: IDS_DESC_LEGGINGS_IRON
data.value: Gives the user 5 Armor when worn.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BOOTS_IRON
data.value: Gives the user 2 Armor when worn.

data.name: IDS_DESC_HELMET_GOLD
data.value: Gives the user 2 Armor when worn.

data.name: IDS_DESC_CHESTPLATE_GOLD
data.value: Gives the user 5 Armor when worn.

data.name: IDS_DESC_LEGGINGS_GOLD
data.value: Gives the user 3 Armor when worn.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BOOTS_GOLD
data.value: Gives the user 1 Armor when worn.

data.name: IDS_DESC_HELMET_DIAMOND
data.value: Gives the user 3 Armor when worn.

data.name: IDS_DESC_CHESTPLATE_DIAMOND
data.value: Gives the user 8 Armor when worn.

data.name: IDS_DESC_LEGGINGS_DIAMOND
data.value: Gives the user 6 Armor when worn.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BOOTS_DIAMOND
data.value: Gives the user 3 Armor when worn.

data.name: IDS_DESC_INGOT
data.value: A shiny ingot which can be used to craft tools made from this material. Created by smelting ore in a furnace.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BLOCK
data.value: Allows ingots, gems, or dyes to be crafted into placeable blocks. Can be used as an expensive building block or compact storage of the ore.

data.name: IDS_DESC_PRESSUREPLATE
data.value: Used to send an electrical charge when stepped on by a player, an animal, or a monster. Wooden Pressure Plates can also be activated by dropping something on them.

data.name: IDS_DESC_SLAB
data.value: Used for making long staircases. Two slabs placed on top of each other will create a normal-sized double slab block.

data.name: IDS_DESC_HALFSLAB
data.value: Used for making long staircases. Two slabs placed on top of each other will create a normal-sized double slab block.

data.name: IDS_DESC_TORCH
data.value: Used to create light. Torches also melt snow and ice.

data.name: IDS_DESC_WOODENPLANKS
data.value: Used as a building material and can be crafted into many things. Can be crafted from any form of wood.

data.name: IDS_DESC_SANDSTONE
data.value: Used as a building material. Is not influenced by gravity like normal Sand.

data.name: IDS_DESC_STICK
data.value: Used to craft torches, arrows, signs, ladders, fences and as handles for tools and weapons.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BED
data.value: Used to forward time from any time at night to morning if all the players in the world are in bed, and changes the spawn point of the player. The colors of the bed are always the same, regardless of the colors of wool used.

data.name: IDS_DESC_CRAFTINGTABLE
data.value: Allows you to craft a more varied selection of items than the normal crafting.

data.name: IDS_DESC_FURNACE
data.value: Allows you to smelt ore, create charcoal and glass, and cook fish and porkchops.

data.name: IDS_DESC_CHEST
data.value: Stores blocks and items inside. Place two chests side by side to create a larger chest with double the capacity.

data.name: IDS_DESC_FENCE
data.value: Used as a barrier that cannot be jumped over. Counts as 1.5 blocks high for players, animals and monsters, but 1 block high for other blocks.

data.name: IDS_DESC_TRAPDOOR
data.value: Activated by using, hitting them or with redstone. They function as normal doors, but are a one by one block and lay flat on the ground.

data.name: IDS_DESC_SIGN
data.value: Shows text entered by you or other players.

data.name: IDS_DESC_GLOWSTONE
data.value: Used to create brighter light than torches. Melts snow/ice and can be used underwater.

data.name: IDS_DESC_TNT
data.value: Used to cause explosions. Activated after placing by igniting with Flint and Steel item, or with an electrical charge.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BOWL
data.value: Used to hold mushroom stew. You keep the bowl when the stew has been eaten.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BUCKET
data.value: Used to hold and transport water, lava and milk.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BUCKET_WATER
data.value: Used to hold and transport water.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BUCKET_LAVA
data.value: Used to hold and transport lava.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BUCKET_MILK
data.value: Used to hold and transport milk.

data.name: IDS_DESC_FLINTANDSTEEL
data.value: Used to create fire, ignite TNT, and open a portal once it has been built.

data.name: IDS_DESC_CLOCK
data.value: Displays positions of the Sun and Moon.

data.name: IDS_DESC_MAP
data.value: Will create an image of an area explored while held. This can be used for path-finding.

data.name: IDS_DESC_MAP_EMPTY
data.value: When used becomes a map of the part of the world that you are in, and gets filled in as you explore.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BOW
data.value: Allows for ranged attacks by using arrows.

data.name: IDS_DESC_NETHER_STAR
data.value: Dropped by the Wither, used in crafting Beacons.

data.name: IDS_DESC_FIREWORKS
data.value: When activated, create colorful explosions. The color, effect, shape and fade are determined by the Firework Star used when the Firework is created.

data.name: IDS_DESC_FIREWORKS_CHARGE
data.value: Used to determine the color, effect and shape of a Firework.

data.name: IDS_DESC_COMPARATOR
data.value: Used in Redstone circuits to maintain, compare, or subtract signal strength, or to measure certain block states.

data.name: IDS_DESC_MINECART_TNT
data.value: Is a type of Minecart that acts as a moving TNT block.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DAYLIGHT_DETECTOR
data.value: Is a block that outputs a Redstone signal based on sunlight (or lack of sunlight).

data.name: IDS_DESC_MINECART_HOPPER
data.value: Is a special type of Minecart that functions similarly to a Hopper. It will collect items lying on tracks and from containers above it.

data.name: IDS_DESC_IRON_HORSE_ARMOR
data.value: A special type of Armor that can be equipped to a horse. Provides 5 Armor.

data.name: IDS_DESC_GOLD_HORSE_ARMOR
data.value: A special type of Armor that can be equipped to a horse. Provides 7 Armor.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DIAMOND_HORSE_ARMOR
data.value: A special type of Armor that can be equipped to a horse. Provides 11 Armor.

data.name: IDS_DESC_LEAD
data.value: Used to leash mobs to the player or Fence posts.

data.name: IDS_DESC_NAME_TAG
data.value: Used to name mobs in the world.

data.name: IDS_DESC_CHICKEN_RAW
data.value: Restores 1{*ICON_SHANK_01*}, or can be cooked in a furnace. Eating this can cause you to be poisoned.

data.name: IDS_DESC_CHICKEN_COOKED
data.value: Restores 3{*ICON_SHANK_01*}. Created by cooking raw chicken in a furnace.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BEEF_RAW
data.value: Restores 1.5{*ICON_SHANK_01*}, or can be cooked in a furnace.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BEEF_COOKED
data.value: Restores 4{*ICON_SHANK_01*}. Created by cooking raw beef in a furnace.

data.name: IDS_DESC_PORKCHOP_RAW
data.value: Restores 1.5{*ICON_SHANK_01*}, or can be cooked in a furnace.

data.name: IDS_DESC_PORKCHOP_COOKED
data.value: Restores 4{*ICON_SHANK_01*}. Created by cooking a raw porkchop in a furnace.

data.name: IDS_DESC_FISH_RAW
data.value: Restores 1{*ICON_SHANK_01*}, or can be cooked in a furnace. Can be fed to an Ocelot to tame it.

data.name: IDS_DESC_FISH_COOKED
data.value: Restores 2.5{*ICON_SHANK_01*}. Created by cooking a raw fish in a furnace.

data.name: IDS_DESC_APPLE
data.value: Restores 2{*ICON_SHANK_01*}, and can be crafted into a golden apple.

data.name: IDS_DESC_GOLDENAPPLE
data.value: Restores 2{*ICON_SHANK_01*}, and regenerates health for 4 seconds. Crafted from an apple and gold nuggets.

data.name: IDS_DESC_ROTTEN_FLESH
data.value: Restores 2{*ICON_SHANK_01*}. Eating this can cause you to be poisoned.

data.name: IDS_DESC_SUGAR
data.value: Used in the cake recipe, and as an ingredient for brewing potions.

data.name: IDS_DESC_LEVER
data.value: Used to send an electrical charge by being turned on or off. Stays in the on or off state until pressed again.

data.name: IDS_DESC_REDSTONETORCH
data.value: Constantly sends an electrical charge, or can be used as a receiver/transmitter when connected to the side of a block. Can also be used for low-level lighting.

data.name: IDS_DESC_REDSTONEREPEATER
data.value: Used in Redstone circuits as repeater, a delayer, and/or a diode.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BUTTON
data.value: Used to send an electrical charge by being pressed. Stays activated for approximately a second before shutting off again.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DISPENSER
data.value: Used to hold and shoot out items in a random order when given a Redstone charge.

data.name: IDS_DESC_NOTEBLOCK
data.value: Plays a note when triggered. Hit it to change the pitch of the note. Placing this on top of different blocks will change the type of instrument.

data.name: IDS_DESC_POWEREDRAIL
data.value: When powered, accelerates minecarts that pass over it. When unpowered, causes minecarts to stop on it.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DETECTORRAIL
data.value: Functions like a Pressure Plate (sends a Redstone signal when powered) but can only be activated by a Minecart.

data.name: IDS_DESC_MINECART
data.value: Used to transport you, an animal, or a monster along rails.

data.name: IDS_DESC_MINECARTWITHCHEST
data.value: Used to transport goods along rails.

data.name: IDS_DESC_MINECARTWITHFURNACE
data.value: Will move along rails and can push other minecarts when coal is put in it.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BOAT
data.value: Used to travel in water more quickly than swimming.

data.name: IDS_DESC_WOOL
data.value: Collected from sheep, and can be colored with dyes.

data.name: IDS_DESC_WOOLSTRING
data.value: Used as a building material and can be colored with dyes. This recipe is not recommended because Wool can be easily obtained from Sheep.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DYE_BLACK
data.value: Used as a dye to create black wool.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DYE_GREEN
data.value: Used as a dye to create green wool.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DYE_BROWN
data.value: Used as a dye to create brown wool, as an ingredient in cookies, or to grow Cocoa Pods.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DYE_SILVER
data.value: Used as a dye to create silver wool.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DYE_YELLOW
data.value: Used as a dye to create yellow wool.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DYE_RED
data.value: Used as a dye to create red wool.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DYE_WHITE
data.value: Used to instantly grow crops, trees, tall grass, huge mushrooms and flowers, and can be used in dye recipes.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DYE_PINK
data.value: Used as a dye to create pink wool.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DYE_ORANGE
data.value: Used as a dye to create orange wool.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DYE_LIME
data.value: Used as a dye to create lime wool.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DYE_GRAY
data.value: Used as a dye to create gray wool.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DYE_LIGHTGRAY
data.value: Used as a dye to create light gray wool. (Note: light gray dye can also be made by combining gray dye with bone meal, letting you make four light gray dyes from every ink sac instead of three.)

data.name: IDS_DESC_DYE_LIGHTBLUE
data.value: Used as a dye to create light blue wool.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DYE_CYAN
data.value: Used as a dye to create cyan wool.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DYE_PURPLE
data.value: Used as a dye to create purple wool.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DYE_MAGENTA
data.value: Used as a dye to create magenta wool.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DYE_BLUE
data.value: Used as dye to create Blue Wool.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DIAMONDS
data.value: Use these to create very strong tools, weapons or armor.

data.name: IDS_DESC_JACKOLANTERN
data.value: Used to create brighter light than torches. Melts snow/ice and can be used underwater.

data.name: IDS_DESC_PAPER
data.value: Used to create books and maps.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BOOK
data.value: Can be used to create bookshelves or enchanted to make Enchanted Books.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BOOKSHELF
data.value: Allows the creation of more powerful enchantments when placed around the Enchantment Table.

data.name: IDS_DESC_ORE_GOLD
data.value: Can be mined with an iron pickaxe or better, then smelted in a furnace to produce gold ingots.

data.name: IDS_DESC_ORE_IRON
data.value: Can be mined with a stone pickaxe or better, then smelted in a furnace to produce iron ingots.

data.name: IDS_DESC_ORE_COAL
data.value: Can be mined with a pickaxe to collect coal.

data.name: IDS_DESC_ORE_LAPIS
data.value: Can be mined with a stone pickaxe or better to collect lapis lazuli.

data.name: IDS_DESC_ORE_DIAMOND
data.value: Can be mined with an iron pickaxe or better to collect diamonds.

data.name: IDS_DESC_ORE_REDSTONE
data.value: Can be mined with an iron pickaxe or better to collect redstone dust.

data.name: IDS_DESC_STONE
data.value: Can be mined with a pickaxe to collect cobblestone.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DIRT
data.value: Collected using a shovel. Can be used for construction.

data.name: IDS_DESC_SAPLING
data.value: Can be planted and it will eventually grow into a tree.

data.name: IDS_DESC_LAVA
data.value: Sets fire to anything that touches it. Can be collected in a bucket.

data.name: IDS_DESC_SAND
data.value: Collected using a shovel. Can be smelted into glass using the furnace. Is affected by gravity if there is no other tile underneath it.

data.name: IDS_DESC_GRAVEL
data.value: Collected using a shovel. Sometimes produces flint when dug up. Is affected by gravity if there is no other tile underneath it.

data.name: IDS_DESC_LOG
data.value: Chopped using an axe, and can be crafted into planks or used as a fuel.

data.name: IDS_DESC_GLASS
data.value: Created in a furnace by smelting sand. Can be used for construction, but will break if you try to mine it.

data.name: IDS_DESC_STONE_BRICK
data.value: Mined from stone using a pickaxe. Can be used to construct a furnace or stone tools.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BRICK
data.value: Baked from clay in a furnace.

data.name: IDS_DESC_CLAY
data.value: Can be baked into bricks in a furnace.

data.name: IDS_DESC_CLAY_TILE
data.value: When broken drops clay balls which can be baked into bricks in a furnace.

data.name: IDS_DESC_SNOW
data.value: A compact way to store snowballs.

data.name: IDS_DESC_TOP_SNOW
data.value: Can be dug with a shovel to create snowballs.

data.name: IDS_DESC_TALL_GRASS
data.value: Sometimes produces wheat seeds when broken.

data.name: IDS_DESC_FLOWER
data.value: Can be crafted into a dye.

data.name: IDS_DESC_MUSHROOM
data.value: Can be crafted with a bowl to make stew.

data.name: IDS_DESC_OBSIDIAN
data.value: Can only be mined with a diamond pickaxe. Is produced by the meeting of water and still lava, and is used to build a portal.

data.name: IDS_DESC_REDSTONE_DUST
data.value: Is placed on the ground to carry an electrical charge. When brewed with a potion it will increase the duration of the effect.

data.name: IDS_DESC_CROPS
data.value: When fully grown, crops can be harvested to collect wheat.

data.name: IDS_DESC_FARMLAND
data.value: Ground that has been prepared ready to plant seeds.

data.name: IDS_DESC_CACTUS
data.value: Can be cooked in a furnace to create a green dye.

data.name: IDS_DESC_REEDS
data.value: Can be crafted to create sugar.

data.name: IDS_DESC_PUMPKIN
data.value: Can be worn as a helmet or crafted with a torch to create a Jack-O-Lantern. It is also the main ingredient in Pumpkin Pie.

data.name: IDS_DESC_HELL_SAND
data.value: Slows the movement of anything walking over it.

data.name: IDS_DESC_PORTAL
data.value: Standing in the portal allows you to pass between the Overworld and the Nether.

data.name: IDS_DESC_COAL
data.value: Used as a fuel in a furnace, or crafted to make a torch.

data.name: IDS_DESC_STRING
data.value: Collected by killing a spider, and can be crafted into a Bow or Fishing Rod, or placed on the ground to create Tripwire.

data.name: IDS_DESC_FEATHER
data.value: Collected by killing a chicken, and can be crafted into an arrow.

data.name: IDS_DESC_SULPHUR
data.value: Collected by killing a Creeper, and can be crafted into TNT or used as an ingredient for brewing potions.

data.name: IDS_DESC_WHEAT_SEEDS
data.value: Can be planted in farmland to grow crops. Make sure there's enough light for the seeds to grow!

data.name: IDS_DESC_WHEAT
data.value: Harvested from crops, and can be used to craft food items.

data.name: IDS_DESC_FLINT
data.value: Collected by digging gravel, and can be used to craft a flint and steel.

data.name: IDS_DESC_SADDLE
data.value: When used on a pig it allows you to ride the pig. The pig can then be steered using a Carrot on a Stick.

data.name: IDS_DESC_SNOWBALL
data.value: Collected by digging snow, and can be thrown.

data.name: IDS_DESC_LEATHER
data.value: Collected by killing a cow, and can be crafted into armor or used to make Books.

data.name: IDS_DESC_SLIMEBALL
data.value: Collected by killing a Slime, and used as an ingredient for brewing potions or crafted to make Sticky Pistons.

data.name: IDS_DESC_EGG
data.value: Dropped randomly by chickens, and can be crafted into food items.

data.name: IDS_DESC_YELLOW_DUST
data.value: Collected by mining Glowstone, and can be crafted to make Glowstone blocks again or brewed with a potion to increase the potency of the effect.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BONE
data.value: Collected by killing a Skeleton. Can be crafted into bone meal. Can be fed to a wolf to tame it.

data.name: IDS_DESC_RECORD
data.value: Collected by getting a Skeleton to kill a Creeper. Can be played in a jukebox.

data.name: IDS_DESC_WATER
data.value: Extinguishes fire and helps crops grow. Can be collected in a bucket.

data.name: IDS_DESC_LEAVES
data.value: When broken sometimes drops a sapling which can then be replanted to grow into a tree.

data.name: IDS_DESC_MOSS_STONE
data.value: Found in dungeons, can be used for construction and decoration.

data.name: IDS_DESC_SHEARS
data.value: Used to obtain wool from sheep and harvest leaf blocks.

data.name: IDS_DESC_PISTON
data.value: When powered (using a button, a lever, a pressure plate, a redstone torch, or redstone with any one of these), a piston extends if it can and pushes blocks.

data.name: IDS_DESC_STICKY_PISTON
data.value: When powered (using a button, a lever, a pressure plate, a redstone torch, or redstone with any one of these), a piston extends if it can and pushes blocks. When it retracts it pulls back the block touching the extended part of the piston.

data.name: IDS_DESC_STONE_BRICK_SMOOTH
data.value: Made from Stone blocks, and commonly found in Strongholds.

data.name: IDS_DESC_IRON_FENCE
data.value: Used as a barrier, similar to fences.

data.name: IDS_DESC_FENCE_GATE
data.value: Similar to a door, but used primarily with fences.

data.name: IDS_DESC_MELON_BLOCK
data.value: Can be crafted from Melon Slices.

data.name: IDS_DESC_THIN_GLASS
data.value: Transparent blocks that can be used as an alternative to Glass Blocks.

data.name: IDS_DESC_PUMPKIN_SEEDS
data.value: Can be planted to grow pumpkins.

data.name: IDS_DESC_MELON_SEEDS
data.value: Can be planted to grow melons.

data.name: IDS_DESC_ENDER_PEARL
data.value: Dropped by Enderman when they die. When thrown, the player will be teleported to the position the Ender Pearl lands at, and will lose some health.

data.name: IDS_DESC_GRASS
data.value: A block of dirt with grass growing on top. Collected using a shovel. Can be used for construction.

data.name: IDS_DESC_SPONGE
data.value: Can be used for construction and decoration.

data.name: IDS_DESC_WEB
data.value: Slows movement when walking through it. Can be destroyed using shears to collect string.

data.name: IDS_DESC_STONE_SILVERFISH
data.value: Spawns a Silverfish when destroyed. May also spawn Silverfish if nearby to another Silverfish being attacked.

data.name: IDS_DESC_VINE
data.value: Grows over time when placed. Can be collected using shears. Can be climbed like a ladder.

data.name: IDS_DESC_ICE
data.value: Slippery when walked on. Turns into water if above another block when destroyed. Melts if close enough to a light source or when placed in The Nether.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BLAZE_ROD
data.value: Used in potion brewing, and for locating Strongholds. Dropped by Blazes who tend to be found near or in Nether Fortresses.

data.name: IDS_DESC_GHAST_TEAR
data.value: Used in potion brewing. Dropped by Ghasts when they die.

data.name: IDS_DESC_GOLD_NUGGET
data.value: Dropped by Zombie Pigmen when they die. Zombie Pigmen can be found in the Nether. Used as an ingredient for brewing potions.

data.name: IDS_DESC_NETHER_STALK_SEEDS
data.value: Used in potion brewing. This can be found naturally growing in Nether Fortresses. It can also be planted on Soul Sand.

data.name: IDS_DESC_POTION
data.value: When used, can have various effects, depending on what it is used on.

data.name: IDS_DESC_GLASS_BOTTLE
data.value: Can be filled with water, and used as the starting ingredient for a potion in the Brewing Stand.

data.name: IDS_DESC_SPIDER_EYE
data.value: This is a poisonous food and brewing item. Dropped when a Spider or Cave Spider is killed by a player.

data.name: IDS_DESC_FERMENTED_SPIDER_EYE
data.value: Used in potion brewing, mainly to create potions with a negative effect.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BLAZE_POWDER
data.value: Used in potion brewing, or crafted with other items to make Eye of Ender or Magma Cream.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BREWING_STAND
data.value: Used for making Potions and Splash Potions.

data.name: IDS_DESC_CAULDRON
data.value: Filled with water by rain or with a bucket of water, and can then be used to fill Glass Bottles with water.

data.name: IDS_DESC_EYE_OF_ENDER
data.value: When thrown, will show the direction to an End Portal. When twelve of these are placed in the End Portal Frames, the End Portal will be activated.

data.name: IDS_DESC_MYCEL
data.value: Similar to Grass Blocks, but very good for growing mushrooms on.

data.name: IDS_DESC_WATERLILY
data.value: Floats on water, and can be walked on.

data.name: IDS_DESC_NETHERBRICK
data.value: Used to build Nether Fortresses. Immune to Ghast's fireballs.

data.name: IDS_DESC_NETHERSTALK
data.value: Found in Nether Fortresses, and will drop Nether Wart when broken.

data.name: IDS_DESC_ENCHANTMENTTABLE
data.value: This allows players to enchant Swords, Pickaxes, Axes, Shovels, Bows and Armor, using the player's Experience Points.

data.name: IDS_DESC_END_PORTAL
data.value: This can be activated using twelve Eye of Ender, and will allow the player to travel to The End dimension.

data.name: IDS_DESC_ENDPORTALFRAME
data.value: Used to form an End Portal.

data.name: IDS_DESC_WHITESTONE
data.value: A block type found in The End. It has a very high blast resistance, so is useful for building with.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DRAGONEGG
data.value: This block is created by the defeat of the Dragon in The End.

data.name: IDS_DESC_EXP_BOTTLE
data.value: When thrown, it drops Experience Orbs which increase your experience points when collected.

data.name: IDS_DESC_FIREBALL
data.value: Useful for setting things on fire, or for indiscriminately starting fires when fired from a Dispenser.

data.name: IDS_DESC_ITEMFRAME
data.value: These are similar to a display case, and will display the item or block placed in it.

data.name: IDS_DESC_MONSTER_SPAWNER
data.value: When thrown can spawn a creature of the type indicated.

data.name: IDS_DESC_WOODSLAB
data.value: Used for making long staircases. Two slabs placed on top of each other will create a normal-sized double slab block.

data.name: IDS_DESC_STONESLAB
data.value: Used for making long staircases. Two slabs placed on top of each other will create a normal-sized double slab block.

data.name: IDS_DESC_ITEM_NETHERBRICK
data.value: Created by smelting Netherrack in a furnace. Can be crafted into Nether Brick blocks.

data.name: IDS_DESC_COCOA
data.value: Can be farmed to collect Cocoa Beans.

data.name: IDS_DESC_SKULL
data.value: Mob Heads can be placed as a decoration, or worn as a mask in the helmet slot.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BEACON
data.value: Projects a beam of light into the sky and can provide Status Effects to nearby players.

data.name: IDS_DESC_CHEST_TRAP
data.value: Stores blocks and items inside. Place two chest side by side to create a larger chest with double capacity. The trapped chest also creates a Redstone charge when opened.

data.name: IDS_DESC_WEIGHTED_PLATE_LIGHT
data.value: Provides a Redstone charge. The charge will be stronger if more items are on the plate.

data.name: IDS_DESC_WEIGHTED_PLATE_HEAVY
data.value: Provides a Redstone charge. The charge will be stronger if more items are on the plate. Requires more weight than the light plate.

data.name: IDS_DESC_REDSTONE_BLOCK
data.value: Used as a redstone power source. Can be crafted back into Redstone.

data.name: IDS_DESC_HOPPER
data.value: Used to catch items or to transfer items into and out of containers.

data.name: IDS_DESC_ACTIVATOR_RAIL
data.value: A type of rail that can enable or disable Minecarts with Hoppers and trigger Minecarts with TNT.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DROPPER
data.value: Used to hold and drop items, or push items into another container, when given a Redstone charge.

data.name: IDS_DESC_STAINED_CLAY
data.value: Colorful blocks crafted by dyeing Hardened clay.

data.name: IDS_DESC_HAY
data.value: Can be fed to Horses, Donkeys or Mules to heal up to 10 Hearts. Speeds up the growth of foals.

data.name: IDS_DESC_HARDENED_CLAY
data.value: Created by smelting Clay in a furnace.

data.name: IDS_DESC_STAINED_GLASS
data.value: Crafted from glass and a dye.

data.name: IDS_DESC_COAL_BLOCK
data.value: A compact way of storing Coal. Can be used as fuel in a Furnace.

data.name: IDS_DESC_COW
data.value: Drops leather when killed. Can also be milked with a bucket.

data.name: IDS_DESC_SHEEP
data.value: Drops wool when sheared (if it has not already been sheared). Can be dyed to make its wool a different color.

data.name: IDS_DESC_CHICKEN
data.value: Drops feathers when killed, and also randomly lays eggs.

data.name: IDS_DESC_PIG
data.value: Drops porkchops when killed. Can be ridden by using a saddle.

data.name: IDS_DESC_WOLF
data.value: Docile until attacked, when they will attack you back. Can be tamed using bones which causes the wolf to follow you around and attack anything that attacks you.

data.name: IDS_DESC_CREEPER
data.value: Explodes if you get too close!

data.name: IDS_DESC_SKELETON
data.value: Fires arrows at you. Drops arrows when killed.

data.name: IDS_DESC_SPIDER
data.value: Attacks you when you are close to it. Can climb walls. Drops string when killed.

data.name: IDS_DESC_ZOMBIE
data.value: Attacks you when you are close to it.

data.name: IDS_DESC_PIGZOMBIE
data.value: Initially docile, but will attack in groups if you attack one.

data.name: IDS_DESC_GHAST
data.value: Fires flaming balls at you that explode on contact.

data.name: IDS_DESC_SLIME
data.value: Split into smaller Slimes when damaged.

data.name: IDS_DESC_ENDERMAN
data.value: Will attack you if you look at it. Can also move blocks around.

data.name: IDS_DESC_SILVERFISH
data.value: Attracts nearby hidden Silverfish when attacked. Hides in stone blocks.

data.name: IDS_DESC_MUSHROOM_COW
data.value: Makes mushroom stew when used with a bowl. Drops mushrooms and becomes a normal cow when sheared.

data.name: IDS_DESC_SNOWMAN
data.value: The Snow Golem can be created by players using snow blocks and a pumpkin. They will throw snowballs at their creators enemies.

data.name: IDS_DESC_ENDERDRAGON
data.value: This is a large black dragon found in The End.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BLAZE
data.value: These are enemies found in the Nether, mostly inside Nether Fortresses. They will drop Blaze Rods when killed.

data.name: IDS_DESC_LAVA_SLIME
data.value: These can be found in The Nether. Similar to Slimes, they will break up into smaller versions when killed.

data.name: IDS_DESC_OZELOT
data.value: These can be found in Jungles. They can be tamed by feeding them Raw Fish. You will need to let the Ocelot approach you though, since any sudden movements will scare it away.

data.name: IDS_DESC_IRONGOLEM
data.value: Appear in Villages to protect them, and can be created using Iron Blocks and Pumpkins.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BAT
data.value: These flying creatures are found in caverns or other large enclosed spaces.

data.name: IDS_DESC_WITCH
data.value: These enemies can be found in swamps and attack you by throwing Potions. They drop Potions when killed.

data.name: IDS_DESC_HORSE
data.value: These animals can be tamed and can then be ridden.

data.name: IDS_DESC_DONKEY
data.value: These animals can be tamed and can then be ridden. They can have a chest attached.

data.name: IDS_DESC_MULE
data.value: Born when a Horse and a Donkey breed. These animals can be tamed and can then be ridden and carry chests.

data.name: IDS_DESC_WITHER
data.value: These are crafted from Wither Skulls and Soul Sand. They fire exploding skulls at you.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BLOCK_GOLD
data.value: A compact way of storing Gold.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BLOCK_IRON
data.value: A compact way of storing Iron.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BLOCK_DIAMOND
data.value: A compact way of storing Diamonds.

data.name: IDS_DESC_BLOCK_LAPIS
data.value: A compact way of storing Lapis Lazuli.

data.name: IDS_ICON_SHANK_03
data.value: &nbsp;<img src="{*IMAGEROOT*}Graphics\Icon_Shank.png" align="middle" height="22" width="22"/><img src="{*IMAGEROOT*}Graphics\Icon_Shank.png" align="middle" height="22" width="22"/><img src="{*IMAGEROOT*}Graphics\Icon_Shank.png" align="middle" height="22" width="22"/>

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_START_TUTORIAL
data.value: {*B*}Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_A*} to start the tutorial.{*B*} Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_B*} if you think you are ready to play on your own.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_OVERVIEW
data.value: Minecraft is a game about placing blocks to build anything you can imagine. At night monsters come out, make sure to build a shelter before that happens.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_LOOK
data.value: Use{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_LOOK*} to look up, down and around.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_SPRINT
data.value: To sprint, push{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_MOVE*} forward twice quickly. While you hold{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_MOVE*} forward, the character will continue to sprint unless they run out of sprint time or food.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_MINE
data.value: Hold{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_ACTION*} to mine and chop using your hand or whatever you are holding. You may need to craft a tool to mine some blocks...

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CHOP_WOOD
data.value: Hold{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_ACTION*} to chop down 4 blocks of wood (tree trunks).{*B*}When a block breaks you can pick it up by standing near to the floating item that appears, causing it to appear in your inventory.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CRAFTING
data.value: Press{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_CRAFTING*} to open the crafting interface.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_INVENTORY
data.value: As you collect and craft more items, your inventory will fill up.{*B*} Press{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_INVENTORY*} to open the inventory.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FOOD_BAR_DEPLETE
data.value: As you move around, mine and attack, you will deplete your food bar{*ICON_SHANK_01*}. Sprinting and sprint jumping use a lot more food than walking and jumping normally.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FOOD_BAR_HEAL
data.value: If you lose some health, but have a food bar with 9 or more{*ICON_SHANK_01*} in it, your health will automatically replenish. Eating food will replenish your food bar.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FOOD_BAR_FEED
data.value: With a food item in your hand, hold{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_USE*} to eat it and replenish your food bar. You cannot eat if your food bar is full.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FOOD_BAR_EAT_STEAK
data.value: Your food bar is low, and you have lost some health. Eat the steak in your inventory to replenish your food bar and start healing.{*ICON*}364{*/ICON*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CREATE_PLANKS
data.value: The wood that you have collected can be crafted into planks. Open the crafting interface to craft them.{*PlanksIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CREATE_CRAFTING_TABLE
data.value: A lot of crafting can involve multiple steps. Now that you have some planks there are more items that you can craft. Create a crafting table.{*CraftingTableIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CREATE_STICKS
data.value: To make collecting blocks faster you can build tools designed for the job. Some tools have a handle made of sticks. Craft some sticks now.{*SticksIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_SCROLL
data.value: Use{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_LEFT_SCROLL*} and{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_RIGHT_SCROLL*} to change the current held item.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_USE
data.value: Use{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_USE*} to use items, interact with objects and place some items. Items that have been placed can be picked up again by mining them with the right tool.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_PLACE_WORKBENCH
data.value: With the crafting table selected, point the crosshair where you want it and use{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_USE*} to place a crafting table.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_OPEN_WORKBENCH
data.value: Point the crosshair at the crafting table and press{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_USE*} to open it.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CREATE_WOODEN_SHOVEL
data.value: A shovel helps dig soft blocks, like dirt and snow, faster. As you collect more materials you can craft tools that work faster and last longer. Create a wooden shovel.{*WoodenShovelIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CREATE_WOODEN_HATCHET
data.value: An axe helps chop wood and wooden tiles, faster. As you collect more materials you can craft tools that work faster and last longer. Create a wooden axe.{*WoodenHatchetIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CREATE_WOODEN_PICKAXE
data.value: A pickaxe helps dig hard blocks, like stone and ore, faster. As you collect more materials you can craft tools that work faster and last longer, and allow you to mine harder materials. Create a wooden pickaxe.{*WoodenPickaxeIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_NIGHT_DANGER
data.value: Night time can approach quickly, and it is dangerous to be outside unprepared. You can craft armor and weapons, but it is sensible to have a safe shelter.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_NEARBY_SHELTER
data.value: Nearby there is an abandoned Miner's shelter that you can complete to be safe overnight.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_COLLECT_RESOURCES
data.value: You will need to collect the resources to complete the shelter. Walls and roof can be made of any tile type, but you will want to create a door, some windows and lighting.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_MINE_STONE
data.value: Use your pickaxe to mine some stone blocks. Stone blocks will produce cobblestone when mined. If you collect 8 cobblestone blocks you can build a furnace. You may need to dig through some dirt to reach the stone, so use your shovel for this.{*StoneIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CREATE_FURNACE
data.value: You have collected enough cobblestone to build a furnace. Use your crafting table to create one.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_PLACE_AND_OPEN_FURNACE
data.value: Use{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_USE*} to place the furnace in the world, and then open it.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CREATE_CHARCOAL
data.value: Use the furnace to create some charcoal. If you are waiting for it to finish how about using the time to collect more materials to finish the shelter?

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CREATE_GLASS
data.value: Use the furnace to create some glass. If you are waiting for it to finish how about using the time to collect more materials to finish the shelter?

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CREATE_WOODEN_DOOR
data.value: A good shelter will have a door so that you can easily go in and out without having to mine and replace the walls. Craft a wooden door now.{*WoodenDoorIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_PLACE_DOOR
data.value: Use{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_USE*} to place the door. You can use{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_USE*} to open and close a wooden door in the world.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CREATE_TORCH
data.value: It can get very dark at night, so you will want some lighting inside your shelter so that you can see. Craft a torch now from sticks and charcoal using the crafting interface.{*TorchIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BASIC_COMPLETE
data.value: You have completed the first part of the tutorial.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_BASIC_COMPLETE
IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_INV_OVERVIEW
data.value: This is your inventory. It shows items available for use in your hand, and all the other items that you are carrying. Your armor is also shown here.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_INV_OVERVIEW
IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_INV_PICK_UP
data.value: Use{*CONTROLLER_MENU_NAVIGATE*} to move the pointer. Use{*CONTROLLER_VK_A*} to pick an item under the pointer. If there is more than one item here this will pick them all up, or you can use{*CONTROLLER_VK_X*} to pick up just half of them.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_INV_MOVE
data.value: Move this item with the pointer over another space in the inventory and place it down using{*CONTROLLER_VK_A*}. With multiple items on the pointer, use{*CONTROLLER_VK_A*} to place them all, or{*CONTROLLER_VK_X*} to place just one.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_INV_DROP
data.value: If you move the pointer outside the edge of the interface with an item on the pointer, you can drop the item.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_INV_INFO
data.value: If you want more information about an item, move the pointer over the item and press{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_MENU_PAGEDOWN*} .

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_INV_EXIT
data.value: Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_B*} now to exit the inventory.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CREATIVE_INV_OVERVIEW
data.value: This is the creative mode inventory. It shows items available for use in your hand, and all the other items that you can choose from.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_CREATIVE_INV_OVERVIEW
IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CREATIVE_INV_PICK_UP
data.value: Use{*CONTROLLER_MENU_NAVIGATE*} to move the pointer. When on the item list, use{*CONTROLLER_VK_A*} to pick an item under the pointer, and use{*CONTROLLER_VK_Y*} to pick up a full stack of that item.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CREATIVE_INV_MOVE
data.value: The pointer will automatically move over a space in the use row. You can place it down using{*CONTROLLER_VK_A*}. Once you have placed the item, the pointer will return to the item list where you can select another item.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CREATIVE_INV_DROP
data.value: If you move the pointer outside the edge of the interface with an item on the pointer, you can drop the item into the world. To clear all items in the quick select bar, press{*CONTROLLER_VK_X*}.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CREATIVE_INV_NAV
data.value: Scroll through the Group Type tabs at the top using{*CONTROLLER_VK_LB*} and{*CONTROLLER_VK_RB*} to select the group type of the item you wish to pickup.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CREATIVE_INV_INFO
data.value: If you want more information about an item, move the pointer over the item and press{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_MENU_PAGEDOWN*} .

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CREATIVE_INV_EXIT
data.value: Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_B*} now to exit the creative mode inventory.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CRAFT_OVERVIEW
data.value: This is the crafting interface. This interface allows you to combine the items you've collected to make new items.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_CRAFT_OVERVIEW
data.value: {*B*}Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_A*} to continue.{*B*} Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_B*} if you already know how to craft.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_PRESS_X_TO_TOGGLE_DESCRIPTION
data.value: {*B*} Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_X*} to show the item description.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_PRESS_X_TO_TOGGLE_INGREDIENTS
data.value: {*B*} Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_X*} to show the ingredients required to make the current item.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_PRESS_X_TO_TOGGLE_INVENTORY
data.value: {*B*} Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_X*} to show the inventory again.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CRAFT_NAV
data.value: Scroll through the Group Type tabs at the top using{*CONTROLLER_VK_LB*} and{*CONTROLLER_VK_RB*} to select the group type of the item you wish to craft, then use{*CONTROLLER_MENU_NAVIGATE*} to select the item to craft.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CRAFT_CREATE
data.value: The crafting area shows the items you require in order to craft the new item. Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_A*} to craft the item and place it in your inventory.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CRAFT_CRAFT_TABLE
data.value: You can craft a larger selection of items using a crafting table. Crafting on a table works in the same way as basic crafting, but you have a larger crafting area allowing more combinations of ingredients.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CRAFT_INVENTORY
data.value: The bottom right part of the crafting interface shows your inventory. This area can also show a description of the currently selected item, and the ingredients required to craft it.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CRAFT_DESCRIPTION
data.value: The description of the currently selected item is now displayed. The description can give you an idea of what the item can be used for.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CRAFT_INGREDIENTS
data.value: The list of ingredients required to craft the selected item are now displayed.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CRAFT_CREATE_PLANKS
data.value: The wood that you have collected can be crafted into planks. Select the planks icon and press{*CONTROLLER_VK_A*} to create them.{*PlanksIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CRAFT_EXIT_AND_PLACE_TABLE
data.value: Now you have built a crafting table you should place it in the world to enable you to build a larger selection of items.{*B*} Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_B*} now to exit the crafting interface.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CRAFT_SELECT_TOOLS
data.value: Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_LB*} and{*CONTROLLER_VK_RB*} to change to the group type of the items you wish to craft. Select the tools group.{*ToolsIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CRAFT_SELECT_STRUCTURES
data.value: Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_LB*} and{*CONTROLLER_VK_RB*} to change to the group type of the items you wish to craft. Select the structures group.{*StructuresIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CRAFT_SELECT_WOODEN_SHOVEL
data.value: Use{*CONTROLLER_MENU_NAVIGATE*} to change to the item you wish to craft. Some items have multiple versions depending on the materials used. Select the wooden shovel.{*WoodenShovelIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CRAFT_SELECT_CRAFTING_TABLE
data.value: A lot of crafting can involve multiple steps. Now that you have some planks there are more items that you can craft. Use{*CONTROLLER_MENU_NAVIGATE*} to change to the item you wish to craft. Select the crafting table.{*CraftingTableIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CRAFT_TOOLS_BUILT
data.value: With the tools you have built you are off to a great start, and are able to collect a variety of different materials more efficiently.{*B*} Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_B*} now to exit the crafting interface.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CRAFT_CREATE_FURNACE
data.value: Some items can not be created using the crafting table, but require a furnace. Craft a furnace now.{*FurnaceIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CRAFT_EXIT_AND_PLACE_FURNACE
data.value: Place the furnace you have crafted in the world. You will want to put this inside your shelter.{*B*} Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_B*} now to exit the crafting interface.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FURNACE_OVERVIEW
data.value: This is the furnace interface. A furnace allows you to change items by firing them. For example, you can turn iron ore into iron ingots in the furnace.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_FURNACE_OVERVIEW
IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FURNACE_METHOD
data.value: You need to put some fuel into the bottom slot of the furnace, and the item to be changed in the top slot. The furnace will then fire up and start working, putting the result in the right-hand slot.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FURNACE_FUELS
data.value: Many wooden items can be used as fuels, but not everything burns for the same time. You may also discover other items in the world that can be used as a fuel.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FURNACE_INGREDIENTS
data.value: When your items have been fired, you can move them from the output area into your inventory. You should experiment with different ingredients to see what you can make.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FURNACE_CREATE_CHARCOAL
data.value: If you use wood as the ingredient then you can make charcoal. Put some fuel in the furnace and wood in the ingredient slot. It can take some time for the furnace to create the charcoal, so feel free to do something else and come back to check the progress.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FURNACE_CHARCOAL_USES
data.value: Charcoal can be used as a fuel, as well as being crafted into a torch with a stick.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FURNACE_CREATE_GLASS
data.value: Placing sand in the ingredient slot allows you to make glass. Create some glass blocks to use as windows in your shelter.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BREWING_MENU_OVERVIEW
data.value: This is the brewing interface. You can use this to create potions that have a variety of different effects.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_BREWING_MENU_OVERVIEW
IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BREWING_MENU_METHOD
data.value: You brew potions by placing an ingredient in the top slot, and a potion or water bottle in the bottom slots (up to 3 can be brewed at one time). Once a valid combination is entered the brewing process will start and create the potion after a short time.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BREWING_MENU_BASIC_INGREDIENTS
data.value: All potions start with a Water Bottle. Most potions are created by first using a Nether Wart to make an Awkward Potion, and will require at least one more ingredient to make the final potion.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BREWING_MENU_EXTENDED_INGREDIENTS
data.value: Once you have a potion you can modify its effects. Adding Redstone Dust increases the duration of its effect and adding Glowstone Dust can make its effect more powerful.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BREWING_MENU_EXTENDED_INGREDIENTS_2
data.value: Adding Fermented Spider Eye corrupts the potion and can turn it into a potion with the opposite effect, and adding Gunpowder turns the potion into a Splash Potion which can be thrown to apply its affect to a nearby area.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BREWING_MENU_CREATE_FIRE_POTION
data.value: Create a Potion of Fire Resistance by first adding Nether Wart to a Water Bottle, and then adding Magma Cream.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BREWING_MENU_EXIT
data.value: Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_B*} now to exit the brewing interface.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BREWING_OVERVIEW
data.value: In this area there is a Brewing Stand, a Cauldron and a chest full of items for brewing.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_BREWING_OVERVIEW
IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BREWING_GET_GLASS_BOTTLE
data.value: The first step in brewing a potion is to create a Water Bottle. Take a Glass Bottle from the chest.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BREWING_FILL_GLASS_BOTTLE
data.value: You can fill a glass bottle from a Cauldron that has water in it, or from a block of water. Fill your glass bottle now by pointing at a water source and pressing{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_USE*}.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BREWING_FILL_CAULDRON
data.value: If a cauldron becomes empty, you can refill it with a Water Bucket.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BREWING_CREATE_FIRE_POTION
data.value: Use the Brewing Stand to create a Potion of Fire Resistance. You will need a Water Bottle, Nether Wart and Magma Cream.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BREWING_USE_POTION
data.value: With a potion in your hand, hold{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_USE*} to use it. For a normal potion you will drink it and apply the effect to yourself, and for a Splash potion you will throw it and apply the effect to creatures near where it hits. Splash potions can be created by adding gunpowder to normal potions.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BREWING_DRINK_FIRE_POTION
data.value: Use your Potion of Fire Resistance on yourself.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BREWING_USE_EFFECTS
data.value: Now that you are resistant to fire and lava, you should see if there are places you can get to that you couldn't before.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_ENCHANTING_MENU_OVERVIEW
data.value: This is the enchanting interface which you can use to add enchantments to weapons, armor and some tools.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_ENCHANTING_MENU_OVERVIEW
IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_ENCHANTING_MENU_START
data.value: To enchant an item, first place it in the enchanting slot. Weapons, armor and some tools can be enchanted to add special effects such as improved damage resistance or increasing the number of items produced when mining a block.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_ENCHANTING_MENU_ENCHANTMENTS
data.value: When an item is placed in the enchanting slot, the buttons on the right will change to show a selection of random enchantments.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_ENCHANTING_MENU_COST
data.value: The number on the button represents the cost in experience levels to apply that enchantment to the item. If you do not have a high enough level the button will be disabled.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_ENCHANTING_MENU_ENCHANT
data.value: Select an enchantment and press{*CONTROLLER_VK_A*} to enchant the item. This will decrease your experience level by the cost of the enchantment.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_ENCHANTING_MENU_BETTER_ENCHANTMENTS
data.value: Although the enchantments are all random, some of the better enchantments are only available when you have a high experience level and have lots of bookcases around the Enchantment Table to increase its power.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_ENCHANTING_OVERVIEW
data.value: In this area there is an Enchantment Table and some other items to help you learn about enchanting.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_ENCHANTING_OVERVIEW
IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_ENCHANTING_SUMMARY
data.value: Using an Enchantment Table allows you to add special effects such as increasing the number of items produced when mining a block, or improved damage resistance for weapons, armor and some tools.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_ENCHANTING_BOOKCASES
data.value: Placing bookcases around the Enchantment Table increases its power and allows access to higher level enchantments.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_ENCHANTING_EXPERIENCE
data.value: Enchanting items costs Experience Levels, which can be built up by collecting Experience Orbs which are produced by killing monsters and animals, mining ores, breeding animals, fishing and smelting/cooking some things in a furnace.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_ENCHANTING_BOTTLE_O_ENCHANTING
data.value: You can also build experience levels using a Bottle O' Enchanting, which, when thrown, creates Experience Orbs around where it lands. These orbs can then be collected.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_ENCHANTING_USE_CHESTS
data.value: In the chests in this area you can find some enchanted items, Bottles O' Enchanting, and some items that have yet to be enchanted for you to experiment with at the Enchantment Table.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_MINECART_OVERVIEW
data.value: You are now riding in a minecart. To exit the minecart, point the cursor at it and press{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_USE*} .{*MinecartIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_MINECART_OVERVIEW
IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_MINECART_RAILS
data.value: A minecart runs on rails. You can also craft a powered minecart with a furnace and a minecart with a chest in it.{*RailIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_MINECART_POWERED_RAILS
data.value: You can also craft powered rails, which take power from redstone torches and circuits to accelerate the cart. These can be connected to switches, levers and pressure plates to make complex systems.{*PoweredRailIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BOAT_OVERVIEW
data.value: You are now sailing a boat. To exit the boat, point the cursor at it and press{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_USE*} .{*BoatIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_BOAT_OVERVIEW
IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BOAT_STEER
data.value: A boat allows you to travel quicker over water. You can steer it using{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_MOVE*} and{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_LOOK*}.{*BoatIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FISHING_OVERVIEW
data.value: You are now using a fishing rod. Press{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_USE*} to use it.{*FishingRodIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_FISHING_OVERVIEW
IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FISHING_CAST
data.value: Press{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_USE*} to cast your line and start fishing. Press{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_USE*} again to reel in the fishing line.{*FishingRodIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FISHING_FISH
data.value: If you wait until the float sinks below the surface of the water before reeling in you can catch a fish. Fish can be eaten raw, or cooked by a furnace, to restore health.{*FishIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FISHING_USES
data.value: As with many other tools a fishing rod has a fixed number of uses. Those uses are not limited to catching fish though. You should experiment with it to see what else can be caught or activated...{*FishingRodIcon*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BED_OVERVIEW
data.value: This is a bed. Press{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_USE*} while pointing at it at night to sleep through the night and awake in the morning.{*ICON*}355{*/ICON*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_BED_OVERVIEW
IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BED_PLACEMENT
data.value: A bed should be placed in a safe, well-lit place so that monsters do not wake you in the middle of the night. Once you have used a bed, if you die you will respawn at that bed. {*ICON*}355{*/ICON*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BED_MULTIPLAYER
data.value: If there are other players in your game, everyone must be in a bed at the same time to be able to sleep. {*ICON*}355{*/ICON*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_REDSTONE_OVERVIEW
data.value: In this area there are some simple Redstone and Piston circuits, and a chest with more items to extend these circuits.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_REDSTONE_OVERVIEW
IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_REDSTONE_POWER_SOURCES
data.value: Levers, Buttons, Pressure Plates and Redstone Torches can all provide power to circuits, either by directly attaching them to the item you want to activate or by connecting them with Redstone dust.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_REDSTONE_POWER_SOURCES_POSITION
data.value: The position and direction that you place a power source can change how it affects the surrounding blocks. For example a Redstone torch on the side of a block can be turned off if the block is powered by another source.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_REDSTONE_DUST
data.value: Redstone dust is collected by mining redstone ore with a pickaxe made of Iron, Diamond or Gold. You can use it to carry power up to 15 blocks, and it can travel up or down one block in height. {*ICON*}331{*/ICON*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_REDSTONE_REPEATER
data.value: Redstone repeaters can be used to extend the distance that the power is carried, or put a delay in a circuit. {*ICON*}356{*/ICON*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_PISTONS
data.value: When powered, a Piston will extend, pushing up to 12 blocks. When they retract, Sticky Pistons can pull back one block of most types. {*ICON*}33{*/ICON*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_TRY_IT
data.value: In the chest in this area there are some components for making circuits with pistons. Try using or completing the circuits in this area, or put together your own. There are more examples outside the tutorial area.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PORTAL_OVERVIEW
data.value: In this area there is a Portal to the Nether!

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_PORTAL_OVERVIEW
IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BUILD_PORTAL
data.value: Portals are created by placing Obsidian blocks into a frame four blocks wide and five blocks tall. The corner blocks are not required.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_ACTIVATE_PORTAL
data.value: To activate a Nether Portal, set fire to the Obsidian blocks inside the frame with a Flint and Steel. Portals can be deactivated if their frame is broken, an explosion happens nearby or a liquid flows through them.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_USE_PORTAL
data.value: To use a Nether Portal, stand inside it. Your screen will go purple and a sound will play. After a few seconds you will be transported to another dimension.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_NETHER
data.value: The Nether can be a dangerous place, full of lava, but can be useful to collect Netherrack which burns forever when lit, and Glowstone which produces light.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_NETHER_FAST_TRAVEL
data.value: The Nether world can be used to fast-travel in the Overworld - traveling one block distance in the Nether is equivalent to traveling 3 blocks in the Overworld.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_CREATIVE_OVERVIEW
data.value: You are now in Creative mode.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_CREATIVE_OVERVIEW
IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CREATIVE_MODE
data.value: When in Creative mode you have in infinite number of all available items and blocks, you can destroy blocks with one click without a tool, you are invulnerable and you can fly.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_OPEN_CREATIVE_INVENTORY
data.value: Press{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_CRAFTING*} to open the creative inventory interface.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CREATIVE_EXIT
data.value: Make your way to the opposite side of this hole to continue.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_CREATIVE_COMPLETE
data.value: You have now completed the Creative mode tutorial.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_FARMING_OVERVIEW
data.value: In this area a farm has been set up. Farming enables you to create a renewable source of food and other items.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_FARMING_OVERVIEW
IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FARMING_SEEDS
data.value: Wheat, Pumpkins and Melons are grown from seeds. Wheat seeds are collected by breaking Tall Grass or harvesting wheat, and Pumpkin and Melon seeds are crafted from Pumpkins and Melons respectively.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FARMING_FARMLAND
data.value: Before planting seeds the dirt blocks need to be turned into Farmland by using a Hoe. A nearby source of water will help keep the Farmland hydrated and make the crops grow faster, as will keeping the area lit.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FARMING_WHEAT
data.value: Wheat goes through several stages when growing, and is ready to be harvested when it appears darker.{*ICON*}59:7{*/ICON*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FARMING_PUMPKIN_AND_MELON
data.value: Pumpkins and Melons also need a block next to where you planted the seed for the fruit to grow once the stem has fully grown.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FARMING_SUGARCANE
data.value: Sugarcane must be planted on a Grass, Dirt or Sand block that is right next to water block. Chopping a Sugarcane block will also drop all blocks that are above it.{*ICON*}83{*/ICON*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FARMING_CACTUS
data.value: Cacti must be planted on Sand, and will grow up to three blocks high. Like Sugarcane, destroying the lowest block will also allow you to collect the blocks that are above it.{*ICON*}81{*/ICON*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FARMING_MUSHROOM
data.value: Mushrooms should be planted in a dimly lit area, and will spread to nearby dimly lit blocks.{*ICON*}39{*/ICON*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FARMING_BONEMEAL
data.value: Bonemeal can be used to grow crops to their fully grown state, or grow Mushrooms into Huge Mushrooms.{*ICON*}351:15{*/ICON*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FARMING_COMPLETE
data.value: You have now completed the farming tutorial.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_BREEDING_OVERVIEW
data.value: In this area animals have been penned in. You can breed animals to produce baby versions of themselves.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_BREEDING_OVERVIEW
IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BREEDING_FEED
data.value: To get the animals to breed, you will need to feed them with the right food to get them to go into 'Love Mode'.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BREEDING_FEED_FOOD
data.value: Feed Wheat to a cow, mooshroom or sheep, Carrots to pigs, Wheat Seeds or Nether Wart to a chicken, or any kind of meat to a wolf, and they'll start looking for another animal of the same species near them that is also in Love Mode.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BREEDING_BABY
data.value: When two animals of the same species meet, and both are in Love Mode, they will kiss for a few seconds, and then a baby animal will appear. The baby animal will follow their parents for a while before growing into a full sized animal itself.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BREEDING_DELAY
data.value: After being in Love Mode, an animal will not be able to enter it again for about five minutes.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BREEDING_FOLLOW
data.value: Some animals will follow you if you are holding their food in your hand. This makes it easier to group animals together to breed them.{*ICON*}296{*/ICON*}

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BREEDING_WOLF_TAMING
data.value: Wild wolves can be tamed by giving them bones. Once tamed Love Hearts will appear around them. Tamed wolves will follow the player and defend them if they haven't been commanded to sit.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BREEDING_COMPLETE
data.value: You have now completed the animal and breeding tutorial.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_GOLEM_OVERVIEW
data.value: In this area are some pumpkins and blocks to make a Snow Golem and an Iron Golem.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_GOLEM_OVERVIEW
IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_GOLEM_PUMPKIN
data.value: Golems are created by placing a pumpkin on top of a stack of blocks.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_GOLEM_SNOW
data.value: Snow Golems are created with two Snow Blocks, one of top of the other, with a pumpkin on top. Snow Golems throw snowballs at your enemies.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_GOLEM_IRON
data.value: Iron Golems are created with four Iron Blocks in the pattern shown, with a pumpkin on top of the middle block. Iron Golems attack your enemies.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_GOLEM_IRON_VILLAGE
data.value: Iron Golems also appear naturally to protect villages, and will attack you if you attack any villagers.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_CONSTRAINT_TUTORIAL_AREA
data.value: You cannot leave this area until you have completed the tutorial.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_HINT_DIGGER_ITEM_SHOVEL
data.value: Different tools are better for different materials. You should use a shovel to mine soft materials like earth and sand.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_HINT_DIGGER_ITEM_HATCHET
data.value: Different tools are better for different materials. You should use an axe to chop tree trunks.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_HINT_DIGGER_ITEM_PICKAXE
data.value: Different tools are better for different materials. You should use a pickaxe to mine stone and ore. You may need to make your pickaxe from better materials to get resources from some blocks.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_HINT_ATTACK_WITH_TOOL
data.value: Certain tools are better for attacking enemies. Consider using a sword to attack.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_HINT_HOLD_TO_MINE
data.value: Hint: Hold {*CONTROLLER_ACTION_ACTION*}to mine and chop using your hand or whatever you are holding. You may need to craft a tool to mine some blocks...

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_HINT_TOOL_DAMAGED
data.value: The tool you are using has become damaged. Every time you use a tool it becomes damaged, and will eventually break. The colored bar below the item in your inventory shows the current damage state.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_HINT_MINECART
data.value: In this area there is a minecart on a track. To enter the minecart, point the cursor at it and press{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_USE*}. Use{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_USE*} on the button to make the minecart move.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_HINT_BOAT
data.value: In the chest beside the river there is a boat. To use the boat, point the cursor at water and press{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_USE*}. Use{*CONTROLLER_ACTION_USE*} while pointing at the boat to enter it.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_HINT_FISHING
data.value: In the chest beside the pond there is a fishing rod. Take the fishing rod from the chest and select it as the current item in your hand to use it.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_HINT_PISTON_SELF_REPAIRING_BRIDGE
data.value: This more advanced piston mechanism creates a self-repairing bridge! Push the button to activate, then investigate how the components interact to learn more.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_HINT_INV_DROP
data.value: If you move the pointer outside of the interface while carrying an item, you can drop that item.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_HINT_CRAFT_NO_INGREDIENTS
data.value: You do not have all the ingredients required to make this item. The box on the bottom left shows the ingredients required to craft this.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_COMPLETED
data.value: Congratulations, you have completed the tutorial. Time in the game is now passing normally, and you don't have long until night time and the monsters come out! Finish your shelter!

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_COMPLETED_EXPLORE
data.value: {*EXIT_PICTURE*} When you are ready to explore further, there is a stairway in this area near the Miner's shelter that leads to a small castle.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_NEW_FEATURES_CHOICE
data.value: New features have been added to the game in the latest version, including new areas in the tutorial world.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_NEW_FEATURES_CHOICE
data.value: {*B*}Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_A*} to play through the tutorial as normal.{*B*} Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_B*} to skip the main tutorial.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_FEATURES_IN_THIS_AREA
data.value: In this area you will find areas setup to help you learn about fishing, boats, pistons and redstone.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_FEATURES_OUTSIDE_THIS_AREA
data.value: Outside of this area you will find examples of buildings, farming, minecarts and tracks, enchanting, brewing, trading, smithing and more!

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_FOOD_BAR_OVERVIEW
data.value: Your food bar has depleted to a level where you will no longer heal.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_FOOD_BAR_OVERVIEW
IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_HORSE_MENU_OVERVIEW
data.value: This is the horse inventory interface.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_HORSE_MENU_OVERVIEW
data.value: {*B*}Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_A*} to continue. {*B*}Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_B*} if you already know how to use the horse inventory.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_HORSE_MENU_LAYOUT
data.value: The horse inventory allows you to transfer, or equip items to your Horse, Donkey or Mule.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_HORSE_MENU_EQUIPMENT
data.value: Saddle your Horse by placing a Saddle in the saddle slot. Horses can be given armor by placing Horse Armor in the armor slot.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_HORSE_MENU_SADDLEBAGS
data.value: You can also transfer items between your own inventory and the saddlebags strapped to Donkeys and Mules in this menu.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_HORSE_OVERVIEW
data.value: {*B*}Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_A*} to learn more about Horses, Donkeys and Mules. {*B*}Press{*CONTROLLER_VK_B*} if you already know about Horses, Donkeys and Mules.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_HORSE_INTRO
data.value: Horses and Donkeys are found mainly in open plains. Mules can be bred from a Donkey and a Horse, but are infertile themselves.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_HORSE_PURPOSE
data.value: All adult Horses, Donkeys and Mules can be ridden. However only Horses can be armored, and only Mules and Donkeys may be equipped with saddlebags for transporting items.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_HORSE_TAMING
data.value: Horses, Donkeys and Mules must be tamed before they can be used. A horse is tamed by attempting to ride it, and managing to stay on the horse while it attempts to throw the rider off.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_HORSE_TAMING2
data.value: When tamed Love Hearts will appear around them and they will no longer buck the player off.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_HORSE_RIDE
data.value: Try to ride this horse now. Use {*CONTROLLER_ACTION_USE*} with no items or tools in your hand to mount it.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_HORSE_SADDLES
data.value: To steer a horse they must then be equipped with a saddle, which can be bought from villagers or found inside chests hidden in the world.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_HORSE_SADDLEBAGS
data.value: Tame Donkeys and Mules can be given saddlebags by attaching a chest. These bags can be accessed whilst riding or when sneaking.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_HORSE_BREEDING
data.value: Horses and Donkeys (but not Mules) can be bred like other animals using Golden Apples or Golden Carrots. Foals will grow into adult horses over time, although feeding them wheat or hay will speed this up.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_HORSE_AREA
data.value: You can try to tame the Horses and Donkeys here, and there are Saddles, Horse Armor and other useful items for Horses in chests around here too.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BEACON_MENU_OVERVIEW
data.value: This is the Beacon interface, which you can use to choose powers for your Beacon to grant.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_BEACON_MENU_OVERVIEW
IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BEACON_MENU_PRIMARY_POWERS
data.value: In the Beacon menu you can select 1 primary power for your Beacon. The more tiers your pyramid has the more powers you will have to choose from.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BEACON_MENU_SECONDARY_POWER
data.value: A Beacon on a pyramid with at least 4 tiers grants an additional option of either the Regeneration secondary power or a stronger primary power.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BEACON_MENU_ACTIVATION
data.value: To set the powers of your Beacon you must sacrifice an Emerald, Diamond, Gold or Iron Ingot in the payment slot. Once set, the powers will emanate from the Beacon indefinitely.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BEACON_OVERVIEW
data.value: At the top of this pyramid there is an inactivate Beacon.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_BEACON_OVERVIEW
IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BEACON_PURPOSE
data.value: Active Beacons project a bright beam of light into the sky and grant powers to nearby players. They are crafted with Glass, Obsidian and Nether Stars, which can be obtained by defeating the Wither.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BEACON_DESIGN
data.value: Beacons must be placed so that they are in sunlight during the day. Beacons must be placed on Pyramids of Iron, Gold, Emerald or Diamond. However the choice of material has no effect on the power of the beacon.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_BEACON_CHOOSING_POWERS
data.value: Try using the Beacon to set the powers it grants, you can use the Iron Ingots provided as the necessary payment.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_PROMPT_HOPPER_OVERVIEW
IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_HOPPER_PURPOSE
data.value: Hoppers are used to insert or remove items from containers, and to automatically pick-up items thrown into them.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_HOPPER_CONTAINERS
data.value: They can affect Brewing Stands, Chests, Dispensers, Droppers, Minecarts with Chests, Minecarts with Hoppers, as well as other Hoppers.

data.name: IDS_TUTORIAL_TASK_HOPPER_MECHANICS
data.value: Hoppers will continuously attempt to suck items out of suitable container placed above them. It will also attempt to insert stored items into an output container.
